purpose: Registry of SharePoint lists that participate in the Operations Follow-up & Task Backbone, and where each one's reflections live (calendar + Planner). The shared sync engine iterates this list. A new operations function joins the backbone by adopting config/followup.contract.json on its list and adding a row here -- no new flow, no new code. See docs/OPERATIONS_FOLLOWUP_BACKBONE.md.

layerStatus.calendar: LIVE — two-way, smoke-tested all 4 directions (create/push/pull/teardown), 0 residue
layerStatus.planner: CODE-COMPLETE + INERT — built into the engine builder (hub-driven one-way), gated on planner.groupId+planId below; verified offline (swagger + structural check). Activate via the consent-session runbook in docs/OPERATIONS_FOLLOWUP_BACKBONE.md s9a

calendarModelNote: Adam chose mailbox delegation (2026-06-21, docs/OPERATIONS_FOLLOWUP_BACKBONE.md s4 UPDATE). Each follow-up event lives on its OWNER'S OWN calendar, written by the standard Office 365 Outlook connector. No central calendar, no attendee trick, no Graph app registration. For operators other than the automation account, the automation account must hold Editor (Add-MailboxFolderPermission) on that operator's calendar and the calendar id is registered in operatorCalendars below.

centralCalendar.note: RETIRED under the delegation model — events go on each owner's own calendar, not a central one.

operatorCalendars.note: Map of operator UPN -> Outlook calendar id reachable by the automation account's connection. The automation account's OWN calendar is the connector default and needs no entry/delegation. Add an entry per delegated operator as they onboard.

planner.note: Dedicated 'CRM Follow-ups' plan in the Guided AI Labs M365 group. Both groupId (the owning M365 group's id) AND planId are required by the Planner connector (CreateTask_V4 + ListBuckets_V3 both take groupId). They are filled in during Adam's consent session (create-connections.js --only=planner,office365users --headed, then create the plan and resolve its id via ListGroupPlans). The engine's Planner layer stays INERT until BOTH are non-null.
planner.bucketNote: by-priority = the engine matches each signal's Priority value to a Planner bucket of the same name (case-insensitive), falling back to the plan's first bucket. Creating named priority buckets is optional polish; the default bucket always works.
